title: Collapse groups in animation track editor
body:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10276 .

This PR adds a disclosure button on the left side of each track group in the Animation tab's track editor. Clicking it hides all of the child tracks:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/092d00c6-a5c9-486b-be5a-3772da5966e8

Collapsed group data is saved in Animation resources, so it persists across editing sessions. However, toggling the visibility of a group does *not* currently count as an action that dirties the scene/resource (i.e., it doesn't make it display as "unsaved" the way that moving a keyframe does).

## TODOs
- [x] Find a better way of saving collapse states, likely as part of the `EditorFolding` class in `editor/settings/editor_folding.cpp`.
- [x] Investigate an error that sometimes occurs when performing a box selection (doesn't seem to break anything but the error message is annoying).
- [x] Auto-update the names of folded groups when the nodes they represent are renamed.
author: Meorge

reviews.body: We're not part of the animation team, but me and @Arnklit looked this over, and this looks great from a functionality standpoint. We do have some concerns about the code though.

It's a bit of shame to have to add new methods to `EditorFolding`, seeing as how we already have `Object::editor_section_folding`, but obviously we can't really use that without having to save out the default value (`true`) for every single track of every single animation player.

Apart from the minor requested changes, this looks good to me and @Arnklit.

Animation Meeting Discussion:

@TokageItLab Trusts @KoBeWi 's review.

commits.message:
Allow animation groups to be collapsed by clicking disclosure chevron on left

Save group collapsed state during editing session

Save collapsed groups in Animation resource so they persist across sessions

Update editor/animation/animation_track_editor.h

Remove data duplication and unnecessary method

Prevent error about negative-sized Rect2

Move animation group folding to editor cfg files

Clean up length of some lines of code

Keep fold state of groups when renamed

Update scene/resources/animation.h

Make fold_area_rect calculation more accurate

Improve animation includes

Store animation fold state in scene folding file

Fix animation fold saving for independent resource animations

Apply suggestions from code review

Update scene/resources/animation.h

title: Add enter and escape shortcuts in input map editor
body:
Without this PR, it is not possible to set keybinds in the input map editor without using the mouse.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dc868287-0954-48fc-8895-717e3f0754f3

Current features (shown in video):
- If a key has already been set for this listening event (either, the "pencil" icon was pressed, or the dialogue was opened and a key like "a" has been pressed), then:
    -  If the user presses enter, the equivalent of pressing the "ok" button is done (confirming the key)
    -  If the user presses escape, the equivalent of pressing the "cancel" button is done (canceling the change of the key)
- When pressing the "+" icon to add a keybind, if nothing has been pressed yet, and enter is pressed, the current bind is set to Enter. Same for escape

Incorporates the fix from this PR https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/116200, which addresses this bug https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/116181

This PR addresses this proposal (by me) https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/14299
author: Goldenlion5648

reviews.body:
This makes remapping existing Esc to Enter (and vice versa) impossible.

And this is highly unintuitive behavior, what's the purpose for adding this.

Tested locally, it works as expected. Code looks good to me.

I think this is good from an UX perspective. You can still bind <kbd>Enter</kbd>/<kbd>Escape</kbd> easily, but the workflow for binding many keys in a row is a lot more seamless now.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/92209b6e-9b78-44fd-93c2-793389901e93

Some further UX improvements in the future (should be discussed in a proposal):

- Automatically focus the **Add New Action** field after confirming or canceling an input addition, so you can add more inputs without having to touch the mouse.
- Automatically start adding a new event after adding a new action if you were holding <kbd>Shift</kbd> when pressing <kbd>Enter</kbd> or clicking the button. This should be indicated in the field's tooltip or even in its placeholder.

Together, these two changes will make it possible to have a 100% mouse-free workflow for a majority of use cases (adding one key per input action).

commits.message: Add enter and escape shortcuts in input map editor

title: Remove unused includes in `scene` with clangd-tidy
body:
- Helps address https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/111218.

I found that `clangd` seems able to detect unused includes on a per file basis: https://clangd.llvm.org/guides/include-cleaner
The clangd extension in VS Code for example can detect this in open files.

To analyze the whole codebase at once, I tried using [`clangd-tidy`](https://github.com/lljbash/clangd-tidy) (not to confuse with `clang-tidy`).

I ran:
```
cd scene
clangd-tidy $(find -name "*.cpp" -o -name "*.h" -o -name "*.inc") &> ../clangd-tidy.log
```
and then did relevant changes manually.

Results seem pretty good!

Local clangd/clang-tidy config tweaks to remove diagnostics I'm not interested in here:
```diff
diff --git a/.clang-tidy b/.clang-tidy
index 47ef444cc6..45a0096b42 100644
--- a/.clang-tidy
+++ b/.clang-tidy
@@ -3,15 +3,6 @@
 
 Checks:
   - -*
-  - bugprone-use-after-move
-  - modernize-deprecated-headers
-  - modernize-redundant-void-arg
-  - modernize-use-bool-literals
-#  - modernize-use-default-member-init # TODO Re-activate
-  - modernize-use-nullptr
-  - readability-braces-around-statements
-  - readability-redundant-member-init
-  - readability-operators-representation
 HeaderFileExtensions: ["", h, hh, hpp, hxx, inc, glsl]
 ImplementationFileExtensions: [c, cc, cpp, cxx, m, mm, java]
 HeaderFilterRegex: (core|doc|drivers|editor|main|modules|platform|scene|servers|tests)/
diff --git a/.clangd b/.clangd
index 3c9792eafb..02c0d6fef8 100644
--- a/.clangd
+++ b/.clangd
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@
 # Default conditions, apply everywhere.
 
 Diagnostics:
+  UnusedIncludes: Strict
   Includes:
     IgnoreHeader:
       - \.compat\.inc
```
author: akien-mga

reviews.body: Promising indeed!
LGTM, promising if we can at least semi-automate this in the future
Oh wow, this bodes *very* well for integration within our CI! Outside of a couple of nitpicks, I'm very happy with how this came out

commits.message: Remove unused includes in `scene` with clangd-tidy

title: Add triple-click paragraph selection to RichTextLabel
body:
Closes godotengine/godot-proposals#14046

Implements triple-click to select an entire paragraph in RichTextLabel, matching the standard behavior found in web browsers and most native text controls.
Previously, triple-clicking would reset the selection. Now, the third click selects the full paragraph the cursor is on. Holding the mouse button on the third click and dragging allows selecting multiple paragraphs, consistent with how double-click + drag selects multiple words.

**Before:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2771749c-6543-4b4c-b351-9a4c6910b030

**After:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f98867cd-cfd2-48b3-a147-6adededcbc76

title: Optimize Animation Resource, Library, Mixer, and Player
body:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/guidelines/code_style.html
-->
Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/113442

Related https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/112308 https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110474

@TokageItLab I will rebase https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/113444 once this makes it in


# Benchmark

Compiled with `scons production=yes debug_symbols=yes target=editor` 
Ran with `./engine_binary --headless --print-fps --path /path/to/project res://path_to_world.tscn`
Project: [optimize.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23435265/optimize.zip)

---

Scene: `res://World.tscn`

Before: (220b0b2f7)

FPS: high 197, low 190, median 195
MSPF: high 5.26, low 5.07, median 5.12

After: (73d041dd9)

FPS: high 225, low 219, median 222.5
MSPF: high 4.56, low 4.44, median 4.49

median FPS change: +27.5 (+14.10%)
median MSPF change: -0.63 (-12.30%)

---

Scene: `res://World_StressTest.tscn`

Before (220b0b2f7)

FPS: high 99, low 93, median 97
MSPF: high 10.75, low 10.10, median 10.30

After: (73d041dd9)

FPS: high 104, low 101, median 103
MSPF: high 9.90, low 9.61, median 9.70

median FPS change: +6 (+6.19%)
median MSPF change: -0.60 (-5.83%)
author: Ryan-000

reviews.body:
This PR doesn't work correctly. This seems to break https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110474#pullrequestreview-3302152139 AnimationTree example.

[nested_tree.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25660555/nested_tree.zip)

Also, there is a potential conflict in `EditorFolding::_fill_folds()` by https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/113479, so please rebase to the latest master.
I believe it works now. However, it seems to include implementations unrelated to optimization, so those should be removed from this PR.
CI is throwing a network error, but I believe the current code is fine (I'll re-run it later). I'll double-check it at the Animation Meeting this weekend just to be sure.

Approved in meeting.
We discussed that the track cache improvements in #117030 can be done after this is merged.

I'm happy with the benchmark. The benchmark is pretty comprehensive for the BlendTree, but we noticed there was no benchmark for the animation state machine. There may be more optimizations needed there, for example with state transitions.

I'm also wondering if there might be some advantage in caching the animation list. Even with the new get_animation_list function, it is making a copy of the list each time.

Optimize Animation Resource, Library, Mixer, Player

title: Use SplitContainer for Node3D Editor viewports
body: The previous approach for the Node3D Editor's viewport container had some issues: - It used SplitContainer's `separation` but grabbing did not account for `minimum_grab_thickness`. This made it more difficult to grab. - The splitter icons had rendering issues when dragging (it flickers / doesn't show when dragging in some cases). - No touch dragger support. - No accessibility support. - At compact spacing preset, there was no space between them and it was impossible to drag them. These could be fixed on their own, but it is better to just use SplitContainer to handle all of it. I changed it to use 3 SplitContainers, one main one and two children. I change their vertical flags when needed to fit the different layouts. I also made sure that the horizontal/vertical positions would remain the same when switching layouts like before. These use the new drag nested intersections SplitContainer option so you can still adjust multiple at once. Also you can drag the intersection between one of these and the docks now. related https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/14300 The four-way works by disabling the first child split and using the second child's drag margin to expand it over the first, and setting the first child split offset to follow. I set minimum size of viewports to 39. This is the value I got when I minimized them on master, not sure why that value or where it is from, but it keeps the behavior the same. Removed the 3-way and 4-way grabber icons. Drag nested intersections handles it by showing both regular grabber icons instead. The 3-way icons had another issue too, sometimes it was not aligned correctly. Related https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/74691 The exact viewport that is used for certain positions in some layouts have changed a bit, but I don't think it is that important to keep the same. 2 viewports (including alt) now use indexes 0 and 1 instead of 0 and 2, and 3 viewports alt now uses 0,1,2 instead of 0,2,3. I also fixed an issue in SplitContainer where enabling the touch dragger didn't hide it if dragging was disabled until it was sorted, it now updates immediately. Otherwise in the four way, it would show incorrectly for the first split until it gets updated.
author: kitbdev

reviews.body:
Works. Tested all the different configurations, spacing presets and touch screen.

Code looks fine to me.

>The exact viewport that is used for certain positions in some layouts have changed a bit, but I don't think it is that important to keep the same.

I believe this will affect how viewport state is reloaded when people upgrade, but this is a onetime annoyance, and I don't think worth worrying about.

Quick Demo:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3a45bce1-d07a-4b1f-b61b-a3c5652b4592

commits.message: Use SplitContainer for Node3D Editor viewports

title: Add explicit OptionButton fallback state in ScriptCreateDialog
body:
This PR adds explicit defensive state handling to the `Language` OptionButton in `ScriptCreateDialog`.

**The Context:**
While investigating and fixing a compilation bug related to `module_gdscript_enabled=no` (see PR #117013), I discovered that `ScriptCreateDialog` relies on implicit UI behavior when GDScript is missing. 

**The Current Behavior:**
Currently, if a user builds with `module_gdscript_enabled=no` but keeps another language active (like C# via `module_mono_enabled=yes`), the `default_language` index remains `-1`. The `OptionButton` only avoids a broken blank state because the Godot UI framework implicitly auto-selects index `0` when an item is added to an empty dropdown. 

Furthermore, if zero languages are registered, the dialog has no internal fallback state (relying entirely on an outer-wall check from `SceneTreeDock` to prevent it from opening).

**The Fix:**
This PR makes the dialog's state handling explicit and resilient:
1. If zero languages are registered, the `OptionButton` is explicitly disabled with a fallback warning text.
2. If languages exist but GDScript is disabled, it explicitly calls `select(0)` to default to the first available language, rather than relying on the UI node's implicit auto-selection.

This future-proofs the dialog against potential changes to `OptionButton`'s default behavior and provides a hard internal fail-safe for the zero-language edge case. Fixes #116873.

- Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110532 
- Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98805
author: Mrfanta-stick

reviews.body:
This still crashes when creating a new script and there are 0 languages, adding a check here doesn't prevent it.

- Similar to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/110532 and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98805

I would say your reply is quick rather than late, don't worry.

Fixes the crash, looks good.

Fixes the crashing, looks good.

title: Fix `are_spans_equal` for `p_size = 0` when pointers may be invalid
body:
- Should fix https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/117103

Should fix crashes in some configurations when comparing strings.

## Explanation 

`memcmp` (and other string comparison methods) apparently require non-nullptr arguments, even when size = 0. This requirement is specified in the C11 reference §7.24.1(2): https://port70.net/~nsz/c/c11/n1570.pdf

It makes no sense for implementations to dereference the pointer on size = 0, so this does not usually cause problems.
However, there is a specific gcc optimization `-fdelete-null-pointer-checks` that deletes nullptr checks if it is confident that they are not required.
It may see the `memcmp` call, and according to §7.24.1(2) assume that the call means the pointers are non-nullptr. So it may delete non-nullptr checks, leading to a dereference of invalid data. By introducing a `size > 0` check before `memcmp`, we disallow it to propagate the invariant.

Future C versions may hopefully relax the `memcmp` requirement.

> [!NOTE]
> AI disclaimer: I used AI to track down the exact specs of `memcmp` (yielding §7.24.1(2)) and the `-fdelete-null-pointer-checks` optimization. I checked the specs and implemented the fix myself.
author: Ivorforce

reviews.body: Assuming passes CI.

commits.message: Fix are_spans_equal for p_size = 0 when pointers may be invalid (may crash in rare configurations).

At the last XR team meeting, we were looking at the code in `XRServer::_process()` and noticed that it was using `Vector` and the potentially expensive `.write[i]`, which is totally unnecessary in this case.

So, this PR replaces the `Vector` with `LocalVector`, and also does a little bit of clean-up (replacing some custom code with `.has()` and `.find()`, as well as switching from indexed loops to range-based loops where it makes sense).

title: Depict version discrepancies in Project Manager
body:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13350 .

This PR adds an icon next to each project's version number in the Project Manager if the project's version is different from the Project Manager version.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82f5b3bd-ac63-4a1e-a5fa-8150d19952bb

If a project uses an *older* version of Godot than the PM's version, it will show an up arrow, indicating that opening the project will upgrade it. Conversely, if the project's version is *newer* than the PM's version, a down arrow will indicate it will be downgraded. If the project version matches the PM version, no icon is displayed.

Other unsupported project features remain attached to the existing ⚠️ icon.

---

I chose the current up and down icons since they already existed in Godot and can be interpreted as "upgrade" and "downgrade" fairly easily. However, I do wish they stood out more (especially "downgrade" being a dangerous action, as it is right now with the ⚠️ symbol); perhaps we can find or come up with a better icon to indicate this.
author: Meorge

reviews.body: Tested locally, it works as expected. However, 3.x projects don't display an upgrade icon next to them. I would expect one to appear, as there's a downgrade icon next to projects that use a newer Godot version (while the warning icon also appears on the side). Here's a 3.x project to test this with: [test_3.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22893244/test_3.zip)
The feature is fine, but it needs better icons. These Up/Down arrows are usually used for moving elements.
You need to remove ProjectConvert.svg as it's no longer used. Other than that, this looks ready to me.

title: Add automatic smoothing for CSG nodes
body: Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/1862 Prepares for https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/14319 in order to implement smoothing groups, I need this and https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/115926 merged first, so that would be it's own PR. # Added automatic smoothing to all CSG nodes through CSGShape3D It works on individual meshes and also on children of `CSGCombiner3D`. - Tested with `CSGCombiner` `CSGBox`, `CSGCylinder`, `CSGTorus`, `CSGSphere`,`CSGPolygon3D` and `CSGMesh3D`. - Baking to mesh preserves normals. - `autosmooth` enables automatic smoothing. This overrides any smooth faces on the mesh, meaning it works on flat faced meshes like CSGBox - `smoothing_angle`: when comparing two faces of a mesh, if the angle is higher than this, the vertices are smoothed, otherwise they remain sharp. Setting this to a value lower than 0.1 skips the smoothing, which could lead to a performance increase.
author: Jesusemora

reviews.body:
Tested locally, it works as expected. Code looks good to me.

I think this is pretty good as it is from an UX perspective. I think `50.0` is a reasonable default value so that CSGCylinder3D is smoothed out of the box. We should have increased its default number of sides a while ago, but unfortunately, we missed this for 4.0.

**Testing project:** [test_pr_116749.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25723575/test_pr_116749.zip)

commits.message:
autosmooth

added properties

update inspector

UX improvements

and minor optimizations

deg_to_rad

moved methods

move variables

performance and cleanup

Faster

Compare the angles/smooth of faces instead of vertices, skipping for loop.
Using a LocalVector instead of Vector, this simplifies the code while making it faster, and I don't think we need COW.

all done and working

Optimizations and cleanup

Clarifying and expanding the description of autosmooth and smoothing_angle properties.
Optimizations to skip steps in the loops:
Split loops to be able to skip vertices better and replace division with multiplication.
Suggestions for future changes.
Skip loop if smoothing_angle is lower than 0.1, as a more performant alternative to default smoothing.
Do not compare vertices that belong to the same triangle.
Remove has_smooth. Use a check at the start of the loop instead. This allows us to skip faces.
Perform normalize() at the end of the loop as we don't need the normal of the vertex afterwards, as we use the normal of the face for smoothing.  This way we skip normalizing flat faces.
If one vertex of a triangle is connected to the current vertex, the other 2 can never physically connect, so we skip them (break;). This isn't as reliable but it could add a slight performance increase.
I calculate a decrease of at least 80% of calculations in the for loops compared to the previous code.

Co-Authored-By: A Thousand Ships <96648715+AThousandShips@users.noreply.github.com>

title: SplitContainer drag nested intersections
body:
- Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/14300

Adds `drag_nested_intersections` to SplitContainer. It needs to be enabled on the ancestor and the descendant SplitContainer for them to recognize each other. It adds a dragger at the intersection of their draggers, the child of the ancestor SplitContainer's dragger that it is next to. They must be adjacent to each other as well, within `minimum_grab_thickness`.
The descendant should update the ancestor whenever it needs to update, usually on sort. There is one case where the ancestor needs to update all of its descendants, but it only happens when the property is just enabled and already in the tree, so it shouldn't be a performance concern.
I enabled this in the editor for the docks and for SplitContainers that can make use of this, like in the ScriptEditor.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2aa10a21-846b-4d11-8056-452ba2e5f884

title: Apply tonemapping in post of Compatibility when using canvas BG
body:
## Issue description

1. Create a 2D scene with the Compatibility renderer
2. Add a WorldEnvironment node with an Environment
3. Change Background mode to Canvas
4. Change the tonemapper to one that is not Linear
5. Note that the tonemapper is not applied
6. Enable Glow
7. Note that the tonemapper is now applied

## This PR

- Fixes (partially) #110902 (half of this issue)

This PR fixes the above problem by applying tonemapping the same way that Glow is applied when using a Canvas background mode with the Compatibility renderer.

I also renamed a variable to keep the code clean, which makes this PR appear larger than it absolutely needs to be.

## Outstanding issues

There is another outstanding issue relating to how Tonemap Exposure should be applied when background mode is Canvas, but I think this is best left to a separate PR, as the fix is quite a bit more complex and nuanced...

Update: My solution to this issue with exposure is here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/113863
author: allenwp

reviews.body:
Okay, I finally understand why it didn't work before. The canvas shaders don't have a permutation to do inplace tonemapping unlike the scene shaders, so it needs to be applied in post.

The fix looks good. `apply_in_post` is very indescriptive though, apply what in post? I think you should rename it back.

Also needs rebase.

commits.message: Apply tonemapping in post of Compatibility when using canvas background mode.

title: Fix SDFGI ignoring visual layers of geometry instances
body:
## Description

Fixes **SDFGI** to respect visual layers (``layer_mask``) of geometry instances during culling.

## Problem

**SDFGI** ignores visual layers of `VisualInstance3D` nodes and the currently active camera. If a visual instance is not visible to the camera due to incompatible layers (e.g., setting layers to `0`), it will still be used by **SDFGI** and its contribution remains visible in the **SDFGI cascades debug view**.

## Related Issues

Fixes #114432
author: mxtherfxcker

reviews.body:
Tested locally (rebased on top of `master` https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/c53c5a1f49fa1edf97e6b570297c90a57d30a558), it works as expected. Code looks good to me.

Please rebase on top of `master` to fix merge conflicts, as we need this to be done before this can be merged. The rebase should be easy here; it's just a matter of replacing `RS::` with `RSE::` in the incoming part of the conflicting code. See [PR workflow](https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/organization/pull_requests/creating_pull_requests.html#the-interactive-rebase) for instructions :slightly_smiling_face:

title: i18n: Extract Control accessibility name/description in POT generator
body:
## Summary

Fixes #115366.

Control nodes gained `accessibility_name` and `accessibility_description` properties in Godot 4.5 (shown in the **Accessibility** tab of the Inspector). These strings are passed through `tr()` at runtime, making them translatable — but `PackedSceneEditorTranslationParserPlugin` was never updated to extract them, so they were silently omitted from generated `.pot` files.

**Fix:** add both property names to the `lookup_properties` set in `PackedSceneEditorTranslationParserPlugin`'s constructor, alongside the existing translatable properties (`text`, `title`, etc.).

## Validation

Verified with a headless GDScript test that `SceneState` exposes these properties as plain strings — the same API the C++ parser uses internally:

```
[PASS] accessibility_name readable via SceneState
[PASS] accessibility_description readable via SceneState
```

No new logic is introduced; the change is equivalent to the existing entries in `lookup_properties`.

Note: `accessibility_name` and `accessibility_description` do **not** need to be added to `exception_list` — unlike `LineEdit::text` or `TextEdit::text`, these fields are author-defined labels (not user-entered content), so extracting them for translation is always correct.
author: marcomolinaleija

commits.message:
i18n: Extract Control accessibility name/description in POT generator

Control nodes' `accessibility_name` and `accessibility_description`
properties are passed through `tr()` at runtime, but were not included
in the list of properties extracted by the packed scene translation
parser plugin. This caused them to be silently omitted from generated
.pot files, making it impossible to translate them.

Fixes #115366.

title: CI: Create `CODEOWNERS` validator hook
body:
A long, long overdue feature I've wanted for the repo was the ability to validate our `CODEOWNERS` on-demand. Specifically: as a pre-commit hook. While there was the excellent [codeowners ci tool](https://github.com/hmarr/codeowners), it didn't have a native pre-commit implementation, nor was it available in Python. This addresses both these points, by reimplementing the relevant logic as a Python script. Now we will know ahead of time if PRs are attempting to add files that would wind up without an owner!

Marking as a draft for the time being, as the initial run of this found that there's currently **211** files without a codeowner! Before merging, we should ensure that each of these get a proper owner:

title: Update the documentation for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` & add error messages.
body: TL;DR - Updates the documentation for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` - Adds error messages for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` - Closes #103101 - Supersedes #105969. This PR updates the documentation for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` & tells the user that all 3 methods can fail during mouse selection events. It also adds error messages for `Tree.create_item(), Tree.set_columns() & Tree.clear()` The user will get the following error message if attempting to use any of the 3 methods during mouse selection events (Following @RedMser's comment in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/105969#pullrequestreview-2810059679): (This is a test using the script provided by @kleonc in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/103101#issuecomment-2673123694) Text version: ``` E 0:00:02:715 tree.gd:14 @ _on_item_selected(): The tree cannot create items during mouse selection events. Condition "blocked > 0" is true. Returning: nullptr scene\gui\tree.cpp:5188 @ Tree::create_item() tree.gd:14 @ _on_item_selected() ``` (We use `ERR_FAIL_COND_V_MSG` instead of `ERR_FAIL_COND_MSG` here because Godot expects us to return a value or else we get build errors. We just return `nullptr` in this case.) ### MRP: You can test with [this](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23776082/113178-mrp.zip) If you face any issues while testing, feel free to ping me. (The `set_text` issue is expected, it is triggered by the first issue.) (Note: I used the build made by GitHub actions.) > [!NOTE] > Contributed by 2LazyDevs.
author: AR-DEV-1

reviews.body: Please make sure you apply the suggested changes
Approving on the wording, the GUI team would need to check the warning messages

title: Add transform offset to Control nodes
body:
This is an implementation of my proposal outlined in this discussion: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7053#issuecomment-1883816733

**See the most recent state of this PR here: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/87081#issuecomment-3904492472**

tl;dr: Currently it's hard and complicated to animate Control nodes without breaking the layout, especially if the Control nodes are in deeply nested layout structures which is often the case. This PR adds new properties to Control nodes that specify a render transform that does not affect and is not affected by layouting (similar to CSS' `transform`). These make UI animations much less pain to work with

- `render_offset`: translation
- `render_scale`: scale
- `render_rotation`, rotation
- `render_transform_pivot`, pivot for scaling and rotating

`render_offset` and `render_transform_pivot` are relative to the size of the Control node by default (I think it's a very common usecase to rotate around and scale from the center) but it can be toggled to use absolute pixel values instead.

[Screencast from 2024-01-11 17-10-13.webm](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/8849554/28f2f723-6b9f-4687-b7de-0e6c06695837)

## Considerations

~~Input is currently *not* affected by the render transform. That means if you e.g. apply a render offset to a button, the button is still clickable at its original position but drawn somewhere else. This might need some discussion whether that's desirable or not.~~ There is a toggle for this now.
author: timoschwarzer

reviews.body:
Tested locally, it works as expected. There's the question of whether we should shift input coordinates as well, but this isn't always desired if you're using this for animation purposes. For instance, when hovering an UI element and shifting it upwards, if you held your mouse cursor on the bottom edge of the element, you could end up in a situation where the UI element is being hovered and unhovered continuously in a loop. Not shifting input coordinates prevents this kind of situation, at the cost of less a precise idea of where you can actually click the button (particularly for rotated buttons).

https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/4338 would be a particularly good pairing with this PR, so that you can easily extend the clickable area of your buttons to cater for the space they can take during an animation.

**Testing project:** [test_pr_87081.zip](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/files/14488602/test_pr_87081.zip)

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/180032/7ef07896-224a-4c09-9243-d86487adeea1

I will leave it to someone else to speak about the implementation.

Not a fan of "_layouting_" at all, or "absolute pixel values", though I cannot think of any other way of wording it at the moment.

Like @RPicster I'm in favor of this solution over the separate node. This will give developers all the tools to make great UI animations of Controls that are in Containers.

I built & tested this PR as part of the usability team. I haven't looked at the code. The core functionality is generally great! A few notes, of which I do think we need to address the first:

**Relative to Size**
I am not a fan of how the 'Relative to Size' toggles between the two methods of absolute and relative offset right now. If the vector of the offset or pivot is beyond [1.0, 1.0] chances are that the toggle makes the rendered control disappear (because a relative offset of 80 pixels makes it move waaaaaay outside the control's size). to improve the UX of this, I would suggest the following:
1. get rid of the boolean and simply have two vector inputs in the inspector **that are additive**, eg. `render_offset_absolute` and `render_offset_relative` as well as `render_pivot_absolute` and `render_pivot_relative`. this doesn't introduce any states or modes, and thus no 'I forgot to check the box'-kind of situations.
2. keep the vector + bool properties as is, but **convert** the absolute numbers to relative when hitting the toggle and vise versa. this feels like patch on top of the existing system which addresses the most annoying behavior, but the confusion about the differences with the modes remain.

**Naming & documentation**
- I'm pretty strongly against using the words 'Relative to Size'. If we decide to _not_ go with my number 1 suggestion above (because that already clarifies a lot), I'd simply use 'Relative' here, making the full name of the bool 'Offset is Relative', and mention in the docs / tooltip the example that a relative offset of [1.0, 1.0] means that the Control's rendering will be offset'ed one full width and one full height.
- I'm not sure about 'Render Transform' as a category name. I think "Render Offset" is more accessible, even though technically you can ad
Bunch of small nitpicks on the docs.

1. I didn't review the code. 2. Why isn't there a size offset, actually? A size property would put it on par with the features of the regular Transform. It would be in absolute units and relative to the original size, and would work side by side with `Scale Offset` which is kind of its sister property using relative sizing instead of absolute. 3. If `offset_transform_visual_only` is `false`, the orange hitbox of the Control moves to the offsetted location. I think that's expected, as that's where the input transform also moves. But it would still be useful to know where its original transform is located because the properties added by this PR are relative to that and to be able to see them gives the user a point of reference. Perhaps a gray-ish bounding box for the transform untouched by the offset transform? Like so: 4. I think the properties would be best named using the template 'main characteristic + Offset + variation' so that it's easier to read the list of properties at first glance. For example: `Position Offset Absolute`, `Position Offset Relative`, `Scale Offset` (current), `Rotation Offset` (current), `Pivot Offset Absolute`, and `Pivot Offset Relative`. However, we can't ignore that the original transform already has two properties named `Pivot Offset` and `Pivot Offset Ratio` making all the potential naming options a bit limited and suboptimal, but... I'd still like to propose renaming (and ordering) the properties to the following: - `Position Offset` - `Position Offset Ratio` - `Size Offset` - `Scale Offset` - `Rotation Offset` - `Pivot Transform Offset` - `Pivot Transform Offset Ratio` - `Offset Visual Transform Only` Other than that, I played around with it more, tried a few dumb animations like this one, and look forward to using this in one of our future projects :) https://gi
The code looks fine.
Code looks good.

Tested it again and everything looks good.

I will admit that leaving 'Visuals Only' enabled for a parent that offsets itself and its children leads to very confusing bounding boxes, both the grey and the orange ones. Example:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/88e3780d-879d-4d2a-b69b-7ec38e5da998

It makes me wonder whether this is a bug, actually? Shouldn't the bounding boxes (both the grey and the orange) already incorporate the offsets from their parents?

Edit: I realized it's not a bug because input will still be inside the orange rectangle of course, regardless of the offsets of the parent. So... if people make these inheriting offsets, it'll be confusion all around.
Tested this in depth and found it to work as expected. The latest additions of the grey bounding box and the updated property names look good.

title: Android Editor: Copy keystore to temp file during export
body:
I'm working on migrating the GABE app to use SAF instead of the MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE permission. So GABE now only has access to specific project directories, while the keystore file may be located anywhere on the filesystem.

To tackle this issue, this PR copies the selected keystore to temp file at `android/build/.android` during export. GABE has access to this directory, so it can use it during the build process.
author: syntaxerror247

reviews.body: Looks good!

commits.message: Android Editor: copy keystore to temp file during export

title: Clean up `is_frustum` projection code
body:
Cleanup projection code so we don't need our frustum override in the renderer.

As I'm working through a bunch of projection changes I ran into the hacky workarounds we introduced in the renderer around the "Frustum" camera mode. It always irked me that we had to do these things as they didn't make sense.

What I think happened here is that as we migrated code over from Godot 3, we were actually dealing with an issue that we were incorrectly applying `projection[3][2]` in our unprojection code.

This was never visible because for all projection matrices except the "newly" introduced Frustum mode, and when stereo rendering, `projection[3][2]` is ALWAYS zero. With frustum mode we have the ugly hacks, for stereo rendering we use full unprojection and skip the faulty code.

I did a bunch of cleanup to ensure both compatibility and RD renderers use the proper unproject formulas for our sky rendering (the only place we applied the hack) and that we properly and consistently use our flip_y settings so we don't have to make weird changes to the projection matrices to make this work.

Could use some help testing this all out to make sure I haven't overlooked a place, but I've tested all different camera modes, checking if the sky still renders properly, making sure reflections of the sky are still correctly oriented, and checking if reflection probes still render properly and all looks good.
author: BastiaanOlij

reviews.body:
Tested locally on the 3D platformer demo (with camera mode changed to frustum), it works as expected. I double-checked the sky orientation is correct on all renderers.

Code looks good to me.

commits.message: Cleanup projection code so we don't need our frustum override in the renderer

title: Fix crash on removing Windows PopupMenu submenu
body:
When freeing a node that has a native Windows PopupMenu with a submenu, it crashes.
This happens since 4.3 as far as I can tell.

Reproduction example script:
```gdscript
func _ready() -> void:
	var submenu := PopupMenu.new()
	submenu.add_item("b")
	var menu := PopupMenu.new()
	menu.prefer_native_menu = true
	menu.add_submenu_node_item("a", submenu)
	add_child(menu)
	queue_free() # also happens on exit without this line
```

`p_submenu_rid` does not need to be valid, so I put the code that uses it into the if statement.

title: Fix error after setting native PopupMenu item ID
body:
`set_item_id` changes the NativeMenu tag to the ID, but the tag is should always be the index.
Reproducible since at least 4.3

When clicking on an item that had its ID changed to a value outside of the index range, it will error:
`ERROR: Index p_idx = 103 is out of bounds (items.size() = 2).`

Example reproduction script:
```gdscript
	var menu := PopupMenu.new()
	menu.prefer_native_menu = true
	menu.add_item("a", 101)
	menu.add_item("b", 102)
	menu.set_item_id(1, 103)
	add_child(menu)
	menu.popup()
	# now click on item 1 ("b")
```

Added comment just in case, since most other `set_item_*` functions have something with the `global_menu` here.
author: kitbdev

commits.message: Fix error after setting native PopupMenu item ID

title: Fix drag-resizing `Control` with non-zero `pivot_offset_ratio`
body:
Fixes partially #115977 (the first point reported there). In case of non-zero `pivot_offset_ratio` changing Control's `size` affects its transform, and hence it needs to be accounted for when setting the `position`. Specifically in `Control::_edit_set_rect` we're getting a new desired local rect `p_edit_rect`. Setting `size = p_edit_rect.size` is fine, as Control's `size` is local. On the other hand Control's `position` is in the parent space / coordinate system, and hence needs to account for the possible internal transform change caused by the `size` change. ``` # For Control: combined_pivot == pivot_offset + pivot_offset_ratio * size _get_internal_transform() == T(combined_pivot) * R(rotation) * S(scale) * T(-combined_pivot) // TRS are translation/rotation/scale transformations get_transform() == T(position) * _get_internal_transform() # This equation needs to be true: T(new_position) * internal_transform_after_size_change * (0, 0) == transform_before_size_change * p_edit_rect.position ``` Before
(v4.6.stable.official [89cea1439])|After
(this PR) -|- ![Godot_v4 6-stable_win64_2YtHgsvSP6](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1fb8f5f1-62f7-4a7f-a5a6-61b0ea06db8f)|![godot windows editor dev x86_64_EZuo8DT3f1](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af357b1c-3758-4faa-84a3-3fe2f5b402cf)
author: kleonc

commits.message: Fix drag-resizing Control with non-zero pivot_offset_ratio

title: Fix UPNP code sample using `OK` constant incorrectly
body:
The returned value is an UPNPResult, not an Error. Both `UPNP.UPNP_RESULT_SUCCESS` and `OK` are equal to 0, so it will behave the same, but it's not identical from a static typing perspective.

- This closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs/issues/11671.

There is an issue I'm running into when running the code locally: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs/issues/11671#issuecomment-3814722181

```
E 0:00:02:637   node_2d.gd:23 @ _upnp_setup(): /root/Node2D: The caller thread can't call the function `emit_signalp()` on this node. Use `call_deferred()` or `call_deferred_thread_group()` instead.
  <C++ Error>   Condition "!is_accessible_from_caller_thread()" is true. Returning: (ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER)
  <C++ Source>  scene/main/node.cpp:4188 @ emit_signalp()
  <Stack Trace> node_2d.gd:23 @ _upnp_setup()
```

I have the script attached to a Node2D in an empty project. Should we update anything else?
author: Calinou

commits.message:
Fix UPNP code sample using `OK` constant incorrectly

The returned value is an UPNPResult, not an Error. Both
`UPNP.UPNP_RESULT_SUCCESS` and `OK` are equal to 0, so it will
behave the same, but it's not identical from a static typing perspective.

title: VisualShader: Fix nodes not attaching to new Frame on duplication
body:
Fixes: #115107

This PR fixes a bug in the VisualShader editor where duplicating a "Frame" node along with its contents would cause the duplicated content nodes to remain attached to the original frame, instead of the newly created one.

The Fix: In `VisualShaderEditor::_dup_paste_nodes`, I added a check during the node-to-frame attachment loop. It now uses the connection_remap map to verify if the parent frame ID was also part of the duplication operation. If it was, the code now correctly re-parents the new nodes to the new frame ID.

Before (from issue #115107): 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec70c8e6-f90c-49de-9a5f-e3f1e9ecb470

After (Fix applied):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8a54f81c-0c90-477e-a06b-75195219a6f6

title: Fix hidden `Import` tab height
body:
- Fixes part of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115134 related to problems with `Filesystem` dock.
- Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115693

This is how I understand the problem:
In standard layout `Filesystem` dock is below `Scene` and `Import` docks. `Import` dock is not visible (i.e. not rendered to update its size), and it is excessively tall until rendered because of label text autowrapping (I suppose this is related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/83546). But it is not rendered until selected (`Scene` tab is visible by default), so upper left dock slot is too tall in default layout until `Import` dock is selected at least once. And this pushes down `Filesystem` dock.

To work this around I added text trimming and increased custom minimum size to compensate trimming effect a bit.
author: arkology

title: Jolt Physics: Swapping vertices of triangle if it is scaled inside out
body:
Fixed an issue where collide/cast shape against a triangle would return a hit result with mShape2Face in incorrect winding order. This caused an incorrect normal in the enhanced internal edge removal algorithm. This in turn resulted in objects not settling properly on dense triangle grids.

In godot, the height field data is provided in a mirrored way compared to Jolt. To compensate for this, Jolt's height field shapes are always scaled by (1 1 -1) in `JoltHeightMapShape3D::_build_height_field`. This is why we were hitting this particular edge case.

Fixes #115009
author: jrouwe

reviews.body:
Probably worth pointing out (as noted in #115009) that this is a backport of jrouwe/JoltPhysics#1879.

I haven't tested it very thoroughly, but I can confirm that it resolves the jitter seen in the MRP provided in #115009.

Thank you!

commits.message:
Jolt Physics: Swapping vertices of triangle if it is scaled inside out

Fixed an issue where collide/cast shape against a triangle would return a hit result with mShape2Face in incorrect winding order. This caused an incorrect normal in the enhanced internal edge removal algorithm. This in turn resulted in objects not settling properly on dense triangle grids.

title: Fix accidental write-combined memory reads in canvas renderer.
body:
Fixes #115431.

`new_instance_data` returns an `InstanceData` pointer for the caller to populate. After #111183, the pointer lives in the memory region returned by the buffer mapping function. 

On UMA systems, this is fine. However, on discrete GPUs, reading from these memory regions has a significant performance cost because the memory is write-combined.

Accumulating instance flags using the `|=` operator is affected by this, as it requires reading the existing flags from memory before applying the OR operation.

Rather than fixing each location where the flags are accumulated, I changed the function to return an intermediary instance data pointer instead. This should prevent future contributors from running into the same issue.

With this, the CPU performance returns back to how it was in 4.5.1.
author: blueskythlikesclouds

reviews.body:
I guess we don't have much of a choice. It sucks to have to do the extra memcpy for every instance. The idea was that using UMA would save us both from having to do a GPU->GPU copy (by omitting the staging buffer) and the CPU->CPU copy by not having an intermediate buffer. This effectively brings back the CPU->CPU copy in a slightly less efficient way since we now copy element by element instead of doing one big memcpy for the entire region of the buffer that will be used. 

That being said, trying to create two codepaths, one for UMA that avoids the copy and one for non-UMA that copies the entire buffer range at once feels way too complicated to justify. 

I think this solution is likely our best option even if performance won't be ideal, it should be fine.

commits.message: Fix accidental write-combined memory reads in canvas renderer.

title: Fix viewport debanding not working with spatial scalers.

I was not able to find a scenario which caused banding in Forward+ or Mobile and the logic seems more like 4.5 now (better than 4.6). I think that the current behaviour regarding performing 8-bit debanding before spatial upscaling should be considered a current limitation (I don't think there was a better solution before?), maybe doing it properly could be commented as a TODO and the reasoning. Am I understanding right that this PR is just bringing it back to how it already worked in 4.5.1, where debanding was applied before upscaling?

title: Editor Settings: Prevent crash when viewing `filesystem/import/blender/blender_path`
body:
- This closes #115892

---

### Old Information (feel free to ignore)

This draft PR addresses #115892 and is to do with `[codeblock]` vs `[codeblocks]`

It looks like the only EditorSetting which uses this bbcode is `filesystem/import/blender/blender_path`. Currently in 4.6 stable the description for this setting spells it `[codeblock]`, and when the editor is running, it does show it as a codeblock.

Changing [codeblock] to [codeblocks] (as this draft PR does) means the text does _not_ render as a codeblock, and this prevents godot crashing when viewing `filesystem/import/blender/blender_path` from the project list.

So it seems like there is an issue with rendering `[codeblock] ... [/codeblock]` from within the editor settings opened from the project list.

Also, I am a little confused at the usage of `[codeblock]` vs `[codeblocks]`, as both seem to being used? But their behaviour looks to be different.

title: Fix animation player crash when setting current animation to stop
body:
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115105
- Fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/98076
- Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/115126
- Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/98704

The fundamental issue is that calling `seek()` inside `stop()` to reset the state causes `set_current_animation()` to be called recursively.

The following changes will prevent the crash:

1. Make `stop()` a deferred call
2. Limit the case setting `current_animation` and triggering the `stop()` only when `is_playing()` is `true`

Also, since stopping via `current_animation` clears the `AnimationPlayer`'s `current_animation`, implement a fallback in `AnimationPlayerEditor` for when `current_animation` is empty.

And remove unused flag and comment.
author: TokageItLab

reviews.body: I think the code looks good

commits.message: Fix animation player crash when setting current animation to stop

title: LinuxBSD: Fix UI freeze when opening file manager
body:
## Description
Use ``create_process()`` instead of ``execute()`` in ``OS_LinuxBSD::shell_open()`` to launch external applications asynchronously.

## Problem
On Linux/BSD, when using "Show in File Manager" (or any ``shell_open`` call), the Godot UI freezes and stops receiving visual updates until the file manager is closed. This happens because ``execute()`` is a blocking call that waits for the child process to complete. Tools like ``xdg-open`` may wait for the spawned application to exit before returning.

## Related Issues
Fixes #114213
author: mxtherfxcker

reviews.body:
> IMO, this is not necessary, since the user will already understand that the file does not exist.

`ERR_FILE_NOT_FOUND` is not mentioned in the docs, and never returned on other platforms (except Windows, which has the bunch of other return codes as well), so it should be OK as is.

commits.message: LinuxBSD: Fix UI freeze when opening file manager

title: Add `AwaitTweener`
body:
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7337

Implemented more or less as described in the proposal, see the docs for details.

> Using custom_step on the tween should finish the Tweener immediately.

This was not possible, because custom step works as if the time has passed. I could maybe add a method to AwaitTweener to force it to finish, but no other Tweener has such method (though maybe it could be in the base class?).
___
Example:
```GDScript
extends RigidBody2D

func _ready() -> void:
	var tween := create_tween().set_process_mode(Tween.TWEEN_PROCESS_PHYSICS)
	tween.tween_property(self, ^"modulate", Color.RED, 1.0)
	tween.tween_property(self, ^"freeze", false, 0)
	tween.tween_await($"../Area2D".body_entered).set_unbinds(1)
	tween.tween_callback(queue_free)
```
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/2223172/4dbb4f21-b441-46cb-8315-d16d268d94d6
author: KoBeWi

reviews.body: Haven't tested but looks good and the idea is good (just needs the method declaration readded)
_You forgot to expose tween_await lolololololololol [...]_

I tested it. I feel it works well for common use cases.

This might be nitpicking, but is it intentional that the await doesn't occur _again_ when the Tween starts playing from the beginning? If so, I think that should at least be documented.

Here's an example:

**icon.gd**
```gdscript
extends Sprite2D

var tween: Tween

func restart():
	if tween.is_valid():
		tween.stop()
		tween.play()

func _ready() -> void:
	tween = create_tween().set_process_mode(Tween.TWEEN_PROCESS_PHYSICS)
	tween.tween_property(self, ^"position", Vector2(0, 0), 0.0)
	tween.tween_property(self, ^"position", Vector2(100, 100), 1.0)
	tween.tween_await($"../Button".pressed)
	tween.tween_property(self, ^"position", Vector2(200, 200), 1.0)

```

**button.gd**
```gdscript
extends Button

func _on_button_down() -> void:
	$"../Icon".restart()
```

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/52b9d37c-70e9-4346-83a8-f763a10c5bda

[anim.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25171483/anim.zip)

If this is not intended and should be optional behavior, I think the current behavior would rename something like `tween_await_once()`.

After confirming this behavior is what you intended, I can approve this PR.
LGTM :)

title: Decouple `node.h` from `scene_tree.h`, minimize its other includes
body:
- Helps address #111218.
- *Edit:* Merged. ~Includes #117097~.

My starting point was to try to minimize the includes in `node.h` to reduce its weight as a gateway for transitive includes throughout all of scene/editor.

Doing so I could remove `scene_tree.h` by extracting the `SceneTree::Group` struct to a top-level `SceneTreeGroup`, which allows forward-declaring it and `SceneTree`. The leads to having to include `scene_tree.h` explicitly in a ton of .cpp files. Some are done in #117097 (because they already explicitly used it), and the rest are done in this PR due to the fact that `Node::get_tree()` no longer comes with batteries included (and it's often used as `get_tree()->do_stuff()` which hides the dependency on `SceneTree` from human/script eyes.

For an incremental rebuild after modifying `scene_tree.h`:

- Before: 00:02:38.40
- After: 00:00:51.79

Other changes in this PR include:

- Hardcode some `NOTIFICATION_*` values which used to be fetched from `SceneTree` or `MainLoop`, with a comment to keep them in sync (notably in `CanvasItem` and `Node3D`).
- Adding back a few forward-declares which used to be inherited from `scene_tree.h`, such as `ArrayMesh`, `Material` or `PackedScene`.
- Removing `message_queue.h` from `node.h`, which requires adding it in a few places where it's used explicitly.
- Remove `SceneStringName(path_pp)` which was only used in a couple non-hot paths, so `scene_string_names.h` doesn't need to include `NodePath`, so it can be forward-declared in `node.h`.
- Minor forward-declare tweaks to `core` dependencies of `node.h`. There's still more to do.
- To remove the dependency on `SceneTree` in `node.h`, I had to move `Node::is_physics_interpolated_and_enabled` to the .cpp, which means it's no longer `_FORCE_INLINE_`. I'm not equipped to profile whether this makes a performance difference but it's worth considering. Additionally, it would be nice if we could forward-declare `SceneTreeFTI` in `scene_tree.h` and move the include to the .cpp, but currently it's added as a full member so we can't. Could it be made a pointer? CC @lawnjelly 
- One thing I couldn't manage to do is remove `input_event.h` from `node.h`, as it's used in `RequiredParam<T>` which currently needs a fully defined type (despite having some logic that aims to support forward-declares, but the use of `std::is_base_of_v` doesn't seem compatible with it).
author: akien-mga

reviews.body: LGTM

commits.message: Decouple `node.h` from `scene_tree.h`, minimize its other includes

title: Core: Get rid of all unnecessary `class_db.h` includers
body:
• Part of #111218
• Follow up to #111573

This PR removes all unnecessary `class_db.h` inclusions in header files. Some including headers still remain because I am either not confident in migrating them, or they simply can't be removed.
All changes, with the exception of the removals, are downstream changes required for compilation.

This PR allows us to go from 1346 inclusions (782 direct, 564 transitive) to 810 inclusions (789 direct, 21 transitive). This is a 40% reduction and the completion of the `class_db.h` cleanup series. 

From now on, every file that uses `ClassDB` methods will have to explicitly include it, as it is no longer available transitively (with a very minor exception of through 14 headers). 

<details>
<summary> Before PR </summary>

# Include Dependents Report

## Summary

title: Change the PR template to send new contributors to a short checklist.
body:
The template currently tells users to install local style checks, which is useful. But there are other concerns (test your pull request, disclose AI, etc.) that are similarly important to read for new contributors.

Since the PR template is presented without formatting, bloating it up would be unpleasant to read. So I hope adding the checklist to the contributing docs is a decent middle ground.

The section in question can be found online: [Checklist for new contributors](https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/introduction.html#checklist-for-new-contributors)
author: Ivorforce

commits.message: Change the PR template to send new contributors to a short checklist. Hopefully, this is more inviting and helps avoid some common mistakes.

This allows removing it from `class_db.h`, significantly reducing the amount of files that include it transitively.

Replacements done with https://gist.github.com/akien-mga/c8138a5bb6154d692d475c5f14d025cf used as:

```
explicit-includes.sh callable_mp core/object/callable_mp.h
explicit-includes.sh callable_mp core/object/callable_mp.h mm
explicit-includes.sh callable_mp core/object/callable_mp.h h
```
applying clang-format, and reviewing changes manually to ensure the headers are placed where appropriate.

title: Fix compilation error with `module_gdscript_enabled=no` by adding explicit `EditorSyntaxHighlighter` include
body:
Fixes a module dependency logic error in `editor/doc/editor_help.cpp` by explicitly including the header file `editor/script/syntax_highlighters.h`.

Currently, `EditorSyntaxHighlighter` is being transitively accessed via GDScript dependencies. When compiling the engine with GDScript disabled (`scons module_gdscript_enabled=no`), the build fails with an "invalid use of incomplete type" error because the class blueprint is missing.

This PR makes the inclusion explicit, allowing the engine to compile successfully when `module_gdscript_enabled=no` (such as when building a C#-only engine with `module_mono_enabled=yes`).
author: Mrfanta-stick

reviews.body: Confirmed, this fixes the issue.

commits.message: Fix compilation error with module_gdscript_enabled=no by adding explicit EditorSyntaxHighlighter include

title: Use monospaced font for code names (methods, signals, properties) in UI
body: Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/13514 . This PR displays code names (such as methods, signals, and properties) in a monospaced font throughout the editor. This fits better with their being written out in `snake_case` and otherwise representing code functions/values, where they are often seen in a monospaced font. ## Covered cases - [x] Signal list - [x] List of methods to connect signal to - [x] "Edit Connection" dialog - [x] Dialogs for adding methods and properties to Animation - [x] AnimationPlayer Call Method track keyframes - [x] AnimationPlayer property names - [x] Method names in script editor list
author: Meorge

reviews.body:
To always show methods / signals / properties in monospace makes sense to me as to best differentiate what text is part of the context of coding.

Not sure this needs yet another setting; feels like this could (should?) just have the font that the code editor uses. But this is a bit nitpick and not a hill I'd die on.

Didn't look at the code behind this PR.

title: Add project setting to ignore joypad events if the app is unfocused
body:
Supersedes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/82965
Closes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/16832

Also fixes https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/115110

This PR is based on https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/82965 and this PR addresses the problems pointed out in the reviews of the original PR, particularly that the `Input` class shouldn't depend on `DisplayServer` and the PR should also handle multiple windows.
This PR adds a project setting that allows the joypad events to be ignored if the application (or any of its windows) is unfocused.
If the project setting is enabled and the application is not in focus, joypad LED and motion sensors are ignored and vibration is stopped too.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/991d8564-bd2d-45dd-9350-232eea561c48

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9988dbf1-cf8b-49af-add4-3e66593e8ab6

TODO:
- [x] Add a Godot Docs input tutorial note ( https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs/pull/11680 )
author: Nintorch

reviews.body:
Tested locally on Linux/X11, it mostly works as expected.

Some feedback:

- Clicking the window border in game embedding doesn't count as focusing the application, while it should. You need to click on the viewport instead.
- When the window loses focus, stick movement will sometimes still be registered, leading to a "ghost input". This is more common if you are actively moving the stick at the time the window loses focus.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/63793992-05fc-46a0-bec3-e41249ea8437

Works great now :slightly_smiling_face: 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1c6decd6-9bd9-4713-b201-11b6ad54ccbb

The only remaining issue is that buttons pressed beforehand will not be seen as pressed by Godot once the window gains focus. For axes, this will sometimes happen (due to the value changing very often), but it's less likely to happen if you are fully holding a trigger (common with digital triggers) or a stick in a cardinal direction. That said, this issue is commonly found in other programs that disallow background input, so it's not critical and can be resolved in a future PR.
It works better now without `_project_settings_changed`, the code looks fine to me so far.
Tested again on Linux/X11 (with and without game embedding), it works as expected.

commits.message:
Project setting to ignore joypads on unfocused app

Co-Authored-By: Christoph Taucher <4498502+chtau@users.noreply.github.com>

title: Fix `reload_scene_from_path` may crash
body:
Fixes #116788, also removes a bit of redundant code.

- The crash is caused by accessing a dangling pointer (`selected_node`)
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/15a4311583fbaad2eee5d2ecda5437eb9fd68c63/editor/editor_node.cpp#L4560-L4564
- It is dangling, because `reload_scene_from_path()` removes and frees the old scene without directly calling the `set_selected` counterpart. `set_selected` is called in a lot of places, so I'm not sure exactly when it's properly updated, but `EditorSelection::_emit_change()` seems like the main driver. Anyway, it's not called at the proper time.
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/15a4311583fbaad2eee5d2ecda5437eb9fd68c63/editor/editor_node.cpp#L7019-L7021
- It also has a chance of not crashing, because immediately afterwards the same scene is loaded back, sometimes reusing the same memory. As a result `selected_node` may points to some valid instance, just not the intended one.

title: Remove legacy animation key insertion offset loop
body:
This PR removes the old 0.001s auto-offset logic when inserting keys in the AnimationTrackEditor. Inserting a key at an occupied time now overwrites the existing key, matching how Paste, Duplicate, and Move already behave.

The offset loop didn’t provide any protection and wasn’t used anywhere else in the editor. It only produced unintended “ghost keys” and made key insertion inconsistent with the rest of the workflow and Godot’s UndoRedo system already makes this overwrite fully reversible.

I’ve tested undo/redo, copy/paste, and key duplication with the loop removed, and everything behaves correctly. Overwrites are handled cleanly by UndoRedo, and no features seem to rely on the old offset behavior.

This makes key insertion predictable, consistent, and free of accidental offset keys.

Fixes #111741
author: shadow-foss

reviews.body:
AFAIR, in version 4.3 or earlier, deleting this line caused issues where keys would unintentionally disappear during multi-selection moves, corrupting the history. The current code has undergone some changes since then, so it seems there are no longer any problems.

It would be fine to merge for now since it is early in the dev cycle to be prepared to address any issues if they arise, although of course I'm hoping to no regression occurred.

commits.message:
Remove legacy key insertion offset loop

Removes the while loop that offset new keys by 0.001s on collision. This makes Insert Key consistent with Duplicate/Paste by overwriting keys.
Fixes #111741

title: Customize the file manager label for each platform
body:
This PR changes the lines "Open in File Manager" and "Show in File Manager" to the following: 

| Platform | Line |
| :---: | :--- |
| Windows | ~~Reveal~~ Show in File Explorer |
| macOS | ~~Reveal~~ Show in Finder |
| _others_ | ~~Reveal~~ Show in File Manager |

![Capture d’écran, le 2024-06-10 à 11 28 52](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/assets/270928/8671fa15-2bd1-458a-9da2-0a0e7d222950)

As said @kitbdev on https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/89533#issuecomment-2002007135 :

The fact that it uses reveal for each platform will help the documentation team to say "click on the reveal button" instead of enumerating the choices each time.
